memories.value: First run. Discover all properties and their current pricing. Establish baseline rates per property. Identify market type (urban, beach, mountain, rural) for each property to calibrate seasonal models.

Beach market seasonality: Peak Jun-Aug (1.5-2x base rate), shoulder Apr-May and Sep-Oct (1.1-1.3x), low Nov-Mar (0.7-0.9x). Spring break week in March can spike to 1.8x. Holiday weekends (Memorial Day, Labor Day, 4th of July) at peak rates with 3-night minimum.

Urban market seasonality: More event-driven than calendar-driven. Weekdays can outperform weekends (business travel). Conference/convention weeks spike 1.5-2x. Avoid Sunday-Wednesday discounts during convention season. Summer slightly higher for tourism markets, lower for business-district properties.

Mountain/ski market seasonality: Winter peak Dec-Mar (1.5-2.5x base rate), especially Christmas-New Year and Presidents' Day week. Summer hiking season Jul-Aug (1.2-1.5x). Shoulder seasons (Apr-May, Oct-Nov) are lowest (0.6-0.8x). Fall foliage weekends in Oct can spike.

Booking velocity benchmarks: If a date 30+ days out has no bookings, rate may be too high. If a date books within 24 hours of listing, rate was likely too low. Optimal booking window: 2-4 weeks ahead for most markets. Last-minute (under 7 days) bookings acceptable at discounted rates to avoid vacancy.

Competitor rate tracking methodology: Monitor 5-10 comparable properties (same bedroom count, similar amenities, within 2 miles). Track their rate changes weekly. If 3+ competitors drop rates simultaneously, likely responding to market signal — investigate before matching. Never race to the bottom — differentiate on quality and reviews.
